(* Contributed by Damien Doligez *)

-------------- MODULE Cantor5_cantor ------------------
THEOREM cantor ==
  \A S, f :
    \E A \in SUBSET S :
      \A x \in S :
        f [x] # A
PROOF OBVIOUS

===============================================

item.text: The module is minimal: it’s the Cantor diagonal theorem only. I’ll check local TLAPS library/examples for set-comprehension proof patterns.

item.text: I’ll patch the theorem with a diagonal-set witness and see which proof obligations TLAPS accepts.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Cantor5_cantor.tla
@@
 THEOREM cantor ==
   \\A S, f :
     \\E A \\in SUBSET S :
       \\A x \\in S :
         f [x] # A
-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  SUFFICES ASSUME NEW S, NEW f
+           PROVE  \\E A \\in SUBSET S :
+                    \\A x \\in S :
+                      f [x] # A
+    OBVIOUS
+  DEFINE A == {x \\in S : x \\notin f[x]}
+  <1>1. A \\in SUBSET S
+    OBVIOUS
+  <1>2. \\A x \\in S : f[x] # A
+    PROOF
+      <2>1. TAKE x \\in S
+      <2>2. ASSUME f[x] = A
+      <2>3. x \\in A <=> x \\notin f[x]
+        BY DEF A
+      <2>4. x \\in A <=> x \\notin A
+        BY <2>2, <2>3
+      <2>5. FALSE
+        BY <2>4
+      <2>6. QED
+        BY <2>2, <2>5
+    QED
+  <1>3. QED
+    BY <1>1, <1>2
 
 ===============================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Cantor5_cantor.tla"

/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'

+  <1>1. A \\in SUBSET S
+    OBVIOUS
+  <1>2. \\A x \\in S : f[x] # A
+    PROOF
+      <2>1. TAKE x \\in S
+      <2>2. ASSUME f[x] = A
+      <2>3. x \\in A <=> x \\notin f[x]
+        BY DEF A
+      <2>4. x \\in A <=> x \\notin A
+        BY <2>2, <2>3
+      <2>5. FALSE
+        BY <2>4
+      <2>6. QED
+        BY <2>2, <2>5

+  <1>3. QED

 ===============================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Cantor5_cantor.tla"

LEMMA IsWellFoundedOnSubrelation ==
       ASSUME NEW S, NEW R, NEW RR, RR \cap (S \X S) \subseteq R,
              IsWellFoundedOn(R,S)
       PROVE  IsWellFoundedOn(RR,S)
<1>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW f \in [Nat -> S],
                      \A n \in Nat : <<f[n+1], f[n]>> \in RR
               PROVE  FALSE
  BY DEF IsWellFoundedOn
<1>2. \A n \in Nat : <<f[n+1], f[n]>> \in RR \cap (S \X S)
  BY <1>1
<1>. QED  BY <1>2 DEF IsWellFoundedOn

(***************************************************************************)
(* If we think of R as a less-than relation on S, then the following is    *)
(* the set of elements of S that are less than x.                          *)
(***************************************************************************)
SetLessThan(x, R, S) ==  {y \in S : <<y, x>> \in R}

(***************************************************************************)
         NEW e \in NonLoopEdges(Nodes)
  PROVE  \E m,n \in Nodes : m # n /\ e = {m,n}
<1>. PICK m,n \in Nodes : e = {m,n}
  BY EdgesAxiom DEF NonLoopEdges
<1>. SUFFICES ASSUME m = n PROVE FALSE
  OBVIOUS
<1>. QED
  BY FS_Singleton, Isa DEF NonLoopEdges

------------------------------------------------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* Here's an informal proof of the following theorem                       *)
(*                                                                         *)

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Cantor5_cantor.tla
@@
 PROOF
-  SUFFICES ASSUME NEW S, NEW f
-           PROVE  \\E A \\in SUBSET S :
-                    \\A x \\in S :
-                      f [x] # A
+  <1>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW S, NEW f
+                 PROVE  \\E A \\in SUBSET S :
+                          \\A x \\in S :
+                            f [x] # A
     OBVIOUS
-  DEFINE A == {x \\in S : x \\notin f[x]}
-  <1>1. A \\in SUBSET S
+  <1> DEFINE A == {x \\in S : x \\notin f[x]}
+  <1>2. A \\in SUBSET S
     OBVIOUS
-  <1>2. \\A x \\in S : f[x] # A
+  <1>3. \\A x \\in S : f[x] # A
     PROOF
       <2>1. TAKE x \\in S
       <2>2. ASSUME f[x] = A
@@
       <2>6. QED
         BY <2>2, <2>5
     QED
-  <1>3. QED
-    BY <1>1, <1>2
+  <1>4. QED
+    BY <1>2, <1>3
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Cantor5_cantor.tla"
